data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-25T00:43:04.262Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+'s **`ExecuteUpdateAsync`** and **`ExecuteDeleteAsync`**. They translate to single SQL statements — no entities loaded into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: bulk UPDATE in a single SQL statement
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s => s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: bulk DELETE in a single SQL statement
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Key points:**
- Generates `UPDATE … SET … WHERE …` / `DELETE FROM … WHERE …` directly in SQL
- Bypasses change tracker entirely — no memory overhead
- No `SaveChangesAsync()` needed (executes immediately)
- **Caveat:** change tracker won't reflect these changes, so any already-tracked entities may be stale. Call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if you continue using the same `DbContext` instance.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about EF Core 8 bulk operations. EF Core 7+ introduced `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync` which translate to SQL `UPDATE` and `DELETE` directly — no entity loading needed.
